Scenario.title: 2015 Ukraine Electric Power Grid Cyberattack
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting Ukraine's electric power grid involved reconnaissance, persistent access, and direct sabotage of industrial control systems, resulting in widespread blackouts.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Shared DB or directory", "Improper configuration", "Industrial control systems", "Custom APIs", "Obsolete products", "Device theft", "Inadequate encryption", "Autonomous system numbers(ASNs)", "Weak passwords", "Internet-facing assets", "SCADA system databases"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Network scanning", "Network sniffing", "Business Email Compromise(BEC)", "Vulnerability exploit", "Memory dumping", "Account takeover", "Malicious firmware update", "RDP exploitation", "Credential harvesting tool", "Encrypted DNS channel", "Malicious attachment", "Scheduled task creation", "Data manipulation"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker performed network scanning to map internet-facing assets, identifying vulnerable SCADA systems and open ports that could serve as entry points into the energy grid infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The threat actor compromised third-party servers or directories to establish command-and-control (C2) infrastructure, enabling covert communication with infected systems within the energy sector.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A spear-phishing campaign targeting engineers with access to industrial networks was executed, leveraging weak password practices and BEC techniques to compromise internal accounts.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-445: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation
stage.description: Malware was deployed through malicious attachments exploiting unpatched custom API endpoints in energy management systems, embedding backdoors into operational technology environments.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A backdoor was installed on misconfigured programmable logic controller (PLC) systems using scheduled tasks, ensuring long-term access to critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: A zero-day vulnerability was exploited in legacy remote terminal unit (RTU) devices to escalate privileges and gain unauthorized administrative access to industrial control systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Attackers manipulated logs in unencrypted traffic channels to obfuscate their activities, making detection of anomalous behavior more difficult within the energy grid's monitoring systems.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Credentials were extracted from stolen engineering laptops using credential harvesting tools, enabling lateral movement across the network and access to restricted systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Using network sniffing techniques, attackers mapped critical substation communication paths by analyzing autonomous system numbers (ASNs), identifying key nodes for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-555: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: Stolen credentials were used to conduct RDP brute-force attacks, allowing the attacker to move laterally between supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Operational data, including grid topology and parameters, was harvested from SCADA system databases through memory-dumping techniques, providing insights for targeted disruptions.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Stolen data was exfiltrated using DNS tunneling over encrypted channels, masking the transfer of sensitive information related to power grid operations.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: A malicious firmware update was deployed to industrial control systems, causing cascading failures in power distribution and triggering widespread outages across the Ukrainian energy grid.

Scenario.summary: The 2015 Ukraine cyberattack leveraged a combination of phishing, malware deployment, and direct sabotage of industrial control systems to disrupt the national power grid, demonstrating advanced persistence and operational sophistication.
